frameworks.name: numerosity tuning
frameworks.one_line: neural populations tuned to numerical magnitude in frontoparietal cortex

frameworks.name: absolute vs relative coding
frameworks.one_line: fixed magnitude tuning contrasted with context-rescaled tuning

frameworks.name: context-dependent neural rescaling
frameworks.one_line: tuning dynamically adapts to the range of numerosities relevant to context

frameworks.name: hierarchical sensory-to-association processing
frameworks.one_line: relative coding emerges as processing advances from sensory to frontoparietal areas

core_question: Do frontoparietal numerosity representations use absolute or context-relative codes, and how does this organize across the visual hierarchy?
key_assumptions: multivariate fMRI patterns reflect underlying tuning properties
neural resources are finite, motivating context-dependent rescaling
manipulations: range of numerosities defining the task context
comparison of representations across early sensory vs frontoparietal regions
key_findings: relative numerosity coding emerges gradually along the processing hierarchy
early sensory areas predominantly exhibit absolute numerosity coding
frontoparietal regions show context-dependent rescaled tuning
interpretations: numerosity representations are organized hierarchically with context-dependent optimization
rescaling enables efficient processing of infinite magnitudes with finite neural resources
